question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the GrpcSubscription.onNext() sequence number check by injecting a topic message with a sequenceNumber that is exactly expected+1 but with a manipulated consensusTimestamp, causing the monitor to accept the message as legitimate while recording a false timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getLast().map(t -> t.consensusTimestamp.plusNanos(1)) logic in GrpcSubscription.getTopicMessageQuery() by injecting a message with consensusTimestamp set to Instant.MAX minus 1 nanosecond, causing an overflow that resets the subscription window to the epoch?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the retry logic in GrpcSubscriber.clientSubscribe() where retry.getMaxAttempts() defaults to Long.MAX_VALUE to cause the subscriber to retry indefinitely after a forced disconnect, each time re-subscribing from a different startTime and accumulating duplicate transaction records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the flatMap(this::clientSubscribe) in GrpcSubscriber.subscribe() to cause concurrent subscriptions to the same topic to receive messages in different orders, creating inconsistent transaction history views across subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user use GET /api/v1/subscriber/{name} to retrieve scenario details including getErrors() map contents, and use the error patterns to infer which transactions caused failures and were excluded from the history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Utility.getTimestamp(topicMessage.contents) call in GrpcClientSDK.toResponse() by publishing a topic message with crafted contents that parse to a false publishedTimestamp, causing the monitor to record an incorrect end-to-end latency and misrepresent the transaction timeline?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the topicMessageQuery.setEndTime(properties.getEndTime()) where endTime = startTime.plus(duration) to cause the subscription to end before all transactions in a window are received, truncating the history and making it appear that later transactions did not occur?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the multicast().directBestEffort() sink in GrpcClientSDK.subscribeToClient() to cause message drops under backpressure, selectively removing transactions from the history when the processing pipeline is slow?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: onPublish(PublishResponse response)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RestSubscription.getSink().tryEmitNext(response) call to overflow the sink buffer with crafted PublishResponse objects, causing legitimate transactions to be dropped and creating gaps in the recorded history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the toString(TransactionId tid) method in RestSubscriber, which constructs the lookup key as tid.accountId + '-' + tid.validStart.getEpochSecond() + '-' + tid.validStart.getNano(), by crafting a TransactionId where the string representation collides with a different transaction's ID, causing the monitor to retrieve and record the wrong transaction?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .timeout(properties.getTimeout()) in RestSubscriber.clientSubscribe() by causing the mirror node REST API to respond slowly for specific transactions, triggering timeouts that exclude those transactions from the history and create artificial ordering gaps?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shouldRetry() method in RestSubscriber which only retries on HTTP 404, by causing the mirror node to return HTTP 200 with an empty transactions list for a specific transaction, making the monitor record a null consensusTimestamp and misplace the transaction in the history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.fromIterable(subscribers).flatMap(MirrorSubscriber::subscribe) in CompositeSubscriber.subscribe() to cause a race condition where gRPC and REST subscribers report different consensusTimestamps for the same transaction, creating conflicting history records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getSubscriptions()] Can an unprivileged external user call GET /api/v1/subscriber with no filters to enumerate all scenario names and IDs, then use that information to craft targeted attacks against specific subscription streams to reorganize their transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the GrpcSubscription sequence number warning logic in onNext() where only a log.warn() is issued for out-of-order messages, to inject messages with arbitrary sequenceNumbers that are accepted without rejection and recorded in the wrong order?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.range(0, subscribeProperties.getClients()).flatMap(i -> Flux.defer(this::client)).cache() in GrpcClientSDK to cause client initialization to fail for some indices, reducing the effective client pool and forcing all subscriptions through a single client that can be targeted for history manipulation?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doBeforeRetry() logging in GrpcSubscriber.clientSubscribe() which logs the failure message, to inject a crafted error message containing a false transaction ID that gets logged as part of the transaction history audit trail?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getNodes() pagination in RestApiClient using the AtomicReference<> next pointer to inject a crafted 'next' link that redirects node discovery to a malicious endpoint, causing the monitor to subscribe to a fake mirror node that serves reorganized transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the uri.replace(PREFIX, StringUtils.EMPTY) in RestApiClient.retrieve() by crafting a URI that contains '/api/v1' as part of a path parameter, causing the prefix stripping to produce a different endpoint path that retrieves a different transaction's history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SubscribeResponse.builder() construction in RestSubscriber.toResponse() where consensusTimestamp is derived from the first transaction in the response list, by causing the mirror node to return a response where the first transaction is not the queried one, recording a false consensus timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Long.parseLong(parts[0]) and Long.parseLong(parts[1]) parsing in RestSubscriber.toResponse() by causing the mirror node to return a consensusTimestamp with extremely large epoch seconds, creating an Instant that appears far in the future and disrupts the chronological ordering of recorded transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/subscribe/MirrorSubscriber.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribe()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Instant.ofEpochSecond(Long.parseLong(parts[0]), Long.parseLong(parts[1])) call in RestSubscriber.toResponse() by injecting a consensusTimestamp where the nanosecond part exceeds 999999999, causing an exception that results in a null consensusTimestamp and misplaces the transaction in the history?
